heading: New career training assistance
label: 911 New career training assistance
notes: Amendments1980—Subsec. (e)(2). inserted reference to State-accredited institutions which have been in existence for not less than two years.Pub. L. 96–254

Change of NameReference to Veterans’ Administration deemed to refer to Department of Veterans Affairs pursuant to , set out as a Department of Veterans Affairs Act note under , Veterans’ Benefits.section 10 of Pub. L. 100–527section 301 of Title 38
amendments: Amendments1980—Subsec. (e)(2). inserted reference to State-accredited institutions which have been in existence for not less than two years.Pub. L. 96–254

children.heading: Eligible employees
children.label: a Eligible employees
children.text: Any employee who elects to receive a separation allowance from the Milwaukee Railroad under an employee protection agreement entered into under shall be entitled to receive from the Board expenses for training in qualified institutions for new career opportunities.section 908 of this title

children.heading: Commencement of training as condition
children.label: b Commencement of training as condition
children.text: To be entitled for assistance under this section, an employee must begin his course of training within two years following the date of his separation from employment with the Milwaukee Railroad.

children.heading: Filing of application; Board determination
children.label: c Filing of application; Board determination
children.text: Entitlement to expenses for assistance under this section shall be determined by the Board on the basis of an application therefor filed by an employee with the Board.

children.heading: Assistance prohibited afterApril 1, 1984
children.label: d Assistance prohibited afterApril 1, 1984
children.text: No assistance may be provided under this section after .April 1, 1984
the term “expenses” means actual expenses paid for room, board, tuition, fees, or educational material in an amount not to exceed $3,000; and

the term “qualified institution” means an educational institution accredited for payment by the Veterans’ Administration under chapter 36 of title 38, or a State-accredited institution which has been in existence for not less than two years.
